claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, I need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me begin with a few questions.

**First, could you please tell me the name(s) of the child or children you're asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who's 5, and Blake, who's 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! So you have two children: Alex (age 5) and Blake (age 10).

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to understand your living situation and the children's circumstances. Let me start with an important question:

**Do both Alex and Blake currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to ask about your residency status, as this is an important factor for Child Benefit eligibility:

**What is your residency status in the UK? Are you a British citizen, settled status holder, or do you have another immigration status?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK - I'm based in Birmingham.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. To properly assess your eligibility, I need a bit more detail about your immigration status:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK? Alternatively, do you have any other specific immigration status such as a visa or residency permit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. That's very helpful.

Now, I need to ask about the children's residency and citizenship:

**Are both Alex and Blake British citizens, or do they have settled status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they're both British citizens.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to ask about your work and residency history:

**Have you been working in the UK, or are you a UK resident? And are you currently working or looking for work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK resident and I live here in Birmingham with my children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to check one more important aspect:

**Are there any other people (such as a partner, another family member, or anyone else) who also lives with you and Alex and Blake, and who might also be claiming Child Benefit for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's just me and the children living together. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I just need to clarify one more thing to be thorough:

**Are Alex and Blake attending school normally, or are they currently in hospital, in local authority care, or in any other special circumstances that might affect their status?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. They're both just going to school normally - Alex is in primary school and Blake is as well.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Before I make my final assessment, let me just verify one more detail:

**Are both Alex and Blake under 16 years old, or is Blake perhaps 16 or 17 and still in full-time education or an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both under 16 - Alex is 5 and Blake is 10, like I mentioned earlier.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules and make my determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news!** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both **Alex** and **Blake**.

Here's a summary of why:

✓ **You are responsible for both children** - they live with you  
✓ **You are a British citizen and UK resident** - the residency requirement is met  
✓ **Both children are British citizens under 16** - they meet the age requirement  
✓ **No special circumstances** - both children are attending school normally with no complications  

You can submit your claim for Child Benefit, and you would receive payments for both children. If you have any further questions or your circumstances change, you should contact HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. Alex is under 16 years old, lives with you (the parent/guardian responsible for them), you are a British citizen and UK resident, and Alex is a British citizen. There are no special circumstances (such as hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. All core eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit. Blake is under 16 years old, lives with you (the parent/guardian responsible for them), you are a British citizen and UK resident, and Blake is a British citizen. There are no special circumstances (such as hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. All core eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. You meet the key eligibility requirements: you are a British citizen and UK resident responsible for both children who live with you; both children are British citizens under 16 years old attending school normally with no special circumstances affecting their status. Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and as the person responsible for both children living with you, you would be the eligible claimant. Note that if your income exceeds £60,000 per year, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this does not affect your eligibility to claim."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. Alex is under 16 years old, lives with you (the parent/guardian responsible for them), you are a British citizen and UK resident, and Alex is a British citizen. There are no special circumstances (such as hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. All core eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit. Blake is under 16 years old, lives with you (the parent/guardian responsible for them), you are a British citizen and UK resident, and Blake is a British citizen. There are no special circumstances (such as hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. All core eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. You meet the key eligibility requirements: you are a British citizen and UK resident responsible for both children who live with you; both children are British citizens under 16 years old attending school normally with no special circumstances affecting their status. Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and as the person responsible for both children living with you, you would be the eligible claimant. Note that if your income exceeds £60,000 per year, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this does not affect your eligibility to claim."
